methodsAttempted.query: site:unibas.ch University of Basel generative AI artificial intelligence teaching assessment ChatGPT guidelines
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the central AI in learning and teaching page, official AI citation PDF, program-specific DMI PDF, research/news items, and an application page.

methodsAttempted.query: site:unibas.ch unibas KI Lehre Studium Pruefungen ChatGPT generative KI Richtlinie
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official German AI teaching page and German AI citation PDF; the German page was treated as a duplicate of the English page for this staged run.

methodsAttempted.query: site:unibas.ch unibas AI privacy data protection ChatGPT University of Basel guidelines
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the English AI citation PDF, central AI teaching page, AI Initiative page, Digital Skills alternative tools page, and University Library access/copyright page.
Direct HTTP fetches confirmed successful access to the four official evidence sources without login, paywall, CAPTCHA, WAF block, or Playwright escalation.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery for the University of Basel found central AI teaching guidance, an official AI citation PDF, the AI Initiative page, and University Library licensed-resource restrictions mentioning ChatGPT and other AI platforms. Duplicate German pages, program-specific guidance, research/news items, and low-specificity digital-skills material were rejected or not used for claims.

targets.sourceTitle: AI Initiative of the University of Basel

stopConditions: Stop after official University of Basel sources support narrow staged claim candidates.
Do not turn program-specific, application-specific, research/news, or duplicate language sources into university-wide policy claims.
Do not mark claims as canonical or human reviewed.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI Initiative of the University of Basel

artifacts.claimText: The University of Basel says it is not planning a general ban on AI-based tools, and that lecturers can decide case by case whether to integrate AI-based tools into teaching, exams, and assessments or restrict their use.

citation.citationTitle: AI in learning and teaching | University of Basel
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The University of Basel does not plan to introduce a general ban on AI-based tools. Professors and lecturers can decide on a case-by-case basis if they want to integrate AI-based tools into their teaching and/or into exams and assessments, or if they want to restrict their use.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Basel states there is no planned general AI-tool ban and leaves teaching, exam, and assessment integration or restriction to case-by-case lecturer decisions.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Basel AI citation guidelines state that AI tools must always be cited like other tools and sources, and that student papers without complete attribution may be seen as attempted plagiarism or cheating.

citation.citationTitle: Guidelines "Citing AI Tools" How to use AI-tools in university studies
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: AI tools must always be cited, just like other tools and sources. Student papers without complete attribution of sources and tools may be seen as attempted plagiarism or cheating.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Basel AI citation guidelines say AI-supported tools may only be used in a supporting role for work submitted for credit, with students retaining a controlling role and responsibility for their own work.

citation.citationTitle: Guidelines "Citing AI Tools" How to use AI-tools in university studies
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Student assignments and examinations must always be the independent work of the students themselves. For this reason, AI-supported tools may only be used in a supporting role for any work that is submitted for credit. Students must retain a controlling role
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guidelines keep student work submitted for credit independent and limit AI tools to a supporting role, with students retaining control.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The initiative provides services that help answer open questions about AI, identify risks and minimise potential disadvantages. artifacts.title: University of Basel AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source crawl for the University of Basel found central AI teaching guidance, an official AI citation PDF, an AI Initiative page, and University Library licensed-resource restrictions naming ChatGPT and other AI platforms. Seven claim candidates were agent-reviewed and kept narrow to the cited official source scope.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Only public official University of Basel or University Library Basel pages were used; no login-gated intranet, private, CAPTCHA-protected, or paywalled sources were accessed.
Program-specific, application-specific, research/news, duplicate German-language, and low-specificity digital-skills sources were rejected or excluded from claim evidence.
AI Initiative evidence is treated as source-status/support-scope information, not as a binding rule for students or staff.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
